learning_objectives: Understand the mobile gaming landscape and market dynamics
Identify key differences between desktop and mobile game development
Compare iOS and Android platform capabilities and constraints
readings: Apple Human Interface Guidelines - Game Design

lecture_notes.introduction: Welcome to Mobile Game Development. Mobile is the largest gaming platform by revenue and player count, and developing for it requires a distinct mindset from desktop or console development.
key_concepts: Mobile gaming market overview and revenue models
iOS vs Android hardware capabilities and fragmentation
Mobile GPU architectures (tile-based rendering)
Memory and storage constraints on mobile devices
Battery life considerations for sustained gameplay
lecture_notes.demonstration: Setting up a Unity project with mobile build targets for both iOS and Android, including configuring player settings and testing with device simulators
setup_requirements: Unity 2023.2 LTS with iOS and Android build support modules
Xcode 15+ for iOS builds (macOS required)
Android Studio with SDK 33+ and NDK
Physical iOS or Android device for testing

review.value: Mobile GPUs process the screen in small tiles to minimize memory bandwidth, which fundamentally affects how shaders and overdraw should be handled
Android spans thousands of device configurations while iOS has a controlled set; both require careful minimum-spec targeting and scalable quality settings

class_schedule.topic: Touch Input Design and Mobile UI Patterns
learning_objectives: Design intuitive touch-based control schemes for games
Apply mobile-specific UI patterns for game interfaces
readings: Touch Interaction Design for Games Ch. 3-5
Unity Input System Documentation - Touch Support

lecture_notes.introduction: Touch input is fundamentally different from keyboard and mouse. Your thumbs occlude the screen, precision is lower, and players expect gesture-based interactions that feel native to the device.
key_concepts: Touch event lifecycle: began, moved, stationary, ended, canceled
Virtual joystick and button placement ergonomics
Multi-touch gesture recognition (pinch, swipe, rotate)
Haptic feedback integration for touch confirmation
Adaptive UI layouts for different screen sizes and aspect ratios
lecture_notes.demonstration: Implementing a virtual joystick with dead zones and a gesture recognizer that handles simultaneous tap, swipe, and pinch inputs using Unity's new Input System
setup_requirements: Unity Input System package installed
Unity project from previous session
Mobile device or touch-capable simulator

review.value: A small circular area at the joystick center where input is ignored, preventing jitter from imprecise thumb placement; typically 10-15% of joystick radius
Touch targets should be at least 44x44 points (Apple) or 48x48 dp (Android) to be reliably tappable; game UI buttons often need to be even larger

learning_objectives: Profile mobile game performance using platform-specific tools
Optimize rendering for mobile GPU architectures
Manage memory and CPU budgets on constrained devices
readings: ARM GPU Best Practices Developer Guide Ch. 4-6

lecture_notes.introduction: Performance is not optional on mobile. Players expect 60fps, thermal throttling can cut your performance in half during extended sessions, and a game that drains the battery will get uninstalled.
key_concepts: Mobile profiling with Unity Profiler, Xcode Instruments, and Android GPU Inspector
Draw call batching and instancing for mobile GPUs
Texture compression formats: ASTC, ETC2, PVRTC
Shader complexity budgets and mobile-friendly alternatives
Thermal throttling detection and adaptive quality scaling
lecture_notes.demonstration: Profiling a sample scene on a physical device, identifying a draw call bottleneck, and reducing it from 400 to under 100 draw calls through static batching and texture atlasing
setup_requirements: Physical mobile device connected for profiling
Unity Profiler configured for remote profiling
Sample scene with intentional performance issues

review.value: Adaptive Scalable Texture Compression supports variable block sizes from 4x4 to 12x12, allowing quality/size tradeoff per texture; universally supported on modern mobile GPUs
After sustained heavy load, mobile devices reduce clock speeds to prevent overheating; games must monitor thermal state and reduce quality proactively to maintain framerate

learning_objectives: Evaluate different mobile game monetization strategies
Implement in-app purchase flows for iOS and Android
Integrate analytics SDKs to track player behavior and revenue metrics
readings: Free-to-Play: Making Money From Games You Give Away Ch. 3-7

lecture_notes.introduction: A great mobile game that nobody can sustain financially is a failed product. Understanding monetization is as important as understanding game design, and analytics is how you measure whether your approach is working.
key_concepts: Monetization models: premium, freemium, ad-supported, hybrid
In-app purchase types: consumable, non-consumable, subscriptions
Ad integration: rewarded video, interstitial, banner placement strategy
Key metrics: ARPDAU, LTV, retention rates, conversion funnels
Ethical monetization and avoiding predatory patterns
lecture_notes.demonstration: Implementing a Unity IAP flow with server-side receipt validation, and integrating Firebase Analytics to track custom events for purchase funnel analysis
setup_requirements: Unity IAP package configured
Apple Developer account with sandbox tester
Google Play Console with test track configured
Firebase project with Analytics enabled

review.value: The total revenue a player generates over their entire engagement with the game; calculated as ARPDAU multiplied by average lifetime in days; must exceed customer acquisition cost for profitability
Server-side verification of purchase receipts with Apple/Google servers to prevent fraud; client-side validation alone is insufficient because the binary can be modified

class_schedule.topic: App Store Optimization and Launch Strategy
learning_objectives: Apply ASO techniques to maximize organic discoverability
Prepare app store listings that convert views to installs
Plan a mobile game launch and soft-launch strategy
readings: App Store Optimization: The Ultimate Guide (2024 Edition)
Apple App Store Review Guidelines - Games Section

lecture_notes.introduction: You can build the best mobile game in the world, but if nobody finds it on the store, it will fail. App Store Optimization is the discipline of making your game discoverable and compelling to potential players.
key_concepts: Keyword research and metadata optimization for app store search
Screenshot and preview video best practices for conversion
Ratings and reviews management strategies
Soft-launch methodology: geography selection, KPI thresholds, iteration cycles
Platform-specific submission requirements and common rejection reasons
lecture_notes.demonstration: Conducting keyword research using App Annie and Sensor Tower, crafting an optimized App Store Connect listing, and reviewing real examples of successful and unsuccessful game store pages
setup_requirements: App Store Connect access
Google Play Console access
Marketing asset creation tools (Figma or equivalent)
Analytics dashboard from previous session

review.value: Releasing in a limited market (e.g., Canada, Australia, Philippines) to gather real player data on retention and monetization before committing to a global launch and marketing spend
The percentage of store page visitors who install the game; optimized through compelling screenshots showing gameplay, clear value proposition in description, and strong ratings above 4.0 stars

learning_objectives: Architect games for cross-platform deployment from a single codebase
Implement platform abstraction layers for input, rendering, and services
Design live operations systems for ongoing content delivery and player engagement
readings: Cross-Platform Game Development with Unity Ch. 8-10
Live Ops Handbook: Running Games as a Service

lecture_notes.introduction: Modern mobile games rarely exist on a single platform, and they are never truly finished at launch. Cross-platform architecture and live operations are what separate a product from a project.
key_concepts: Platform abstraction patterns: service locator and dependency injection
Conditional compilation and platform-specific code paths
Remote configuration and feature flags for live tuning
Live event systems: time-limited content, seasonal events, battle passes
Over-the-air content updates using addressable assets
lecture_notes.demonstration: Building a platform abstraction layer that handles input, ads, and IAP differently on iOS vs Android from a single codebase, then setting up Firebase Remote Config to tune game parameters without an app update
setup_requirements: Unity project with both iOS and Android targets
Firebase Remote Config configured
Unity Addressables package installed
Version control with branching strategy for live updates

review.value: Unity's system for loading assets by address string rather than direct reference, enabling on-demand downloading of content bundles after initial install to reduce app size and support live content updates
Server-controlled booleans or values that enable or disable features at runtime without requiring an app update; critical for A/B testing, gradual rollouts, and emergency kill switches

quizzes.topic: Mobile Platforms, Touch Input, and Performance

questions.question: A mobile game runs at a stable 60fps for the first 10 minutes of play, then gradually drops to 35fps over the next 20 minutes with no change in scene complexity. The device surface is noticeably warm. Explain the most likely cause and describe two concrete strategies the developer should implement to address this problem.
questions.answer: The most likely cause is thermal throttling. As the device heats up from sustained GPU/CPU load, the operating system reduces clock speeds to prevent hardware damage, resulting in lower frame rates despite constant scene complexity. Two strategies: (1) Implement an adaptive quality system that monitors device thermal state (using iOS ProcessInfo.thermalState or Android PowerManager) and dynamically reduces rendering resolution, shadow quality, or particle counts when thermal pressure increases. (2) Reduce sustained GPU load by using more aggressive LOD transitions, lowering the render resolution to 85-90% and upscaling, and ensuring shaders avoid expensive operations like dependent texture reads in fragment shaders.

rubric.key: Correctly identifies thermal throttling as the cause
First mitigation strategy is concrete and technically sound
Second mitigation strategy is concrete and technically sound

questions.question: Explain why ASTC is generally preferred over ETC2 and PVRTC for mobile texture compression in 2024, and describe one scenario where you might still choose ETC2.
questions.answer: ASTC is preferred because it offers variable block sizes (4x4 to 12x12 texels) allowing developers to choose the quality-to-size ratio per texture, it supports both LDR and HDR content, and it handles all texture types (color, normals, masks) with good quality. It is universally supported on modern iOS (A8+) and Android (OpenGL ES 3.2+) devices. You might still choose ETC2 when targeting very old Android devices running OpenGL ES 3.0 that lack ASTC support but do support ETC2, as ETC2 is mandatory in the OpenGL ES 3.0 specification.

rubric.key: Explains ASTC variable block size advantage
Mentions ASTC universal support on modern devices
Valid scenario for choosing ETC2 (legacy device support)

questions.question: A free-to-play mobile game has Day 1 retention of 40%, Day 7 retention of 15%, Day 30 retention of 5%, and an ARPDAU of $0.08. Calculate the approximate LTV of an average player assuming the retention curve is roughly exponential, and explain whether the game can profitably acquire users at a CPI (cost per install) of $2.00.
questions.answer: Using the area-under-the-curve method with the given retention points, the estimated average player lifetime is approximately 15-20 days. LTV = ARPDAU x average lifetime = $0.08 x ~17 = ~$1.36. At a CPI of $2.00, the game cannot profitably acquire users because LTV ($1.36) is less than CPI ($2.00). The LTV/CPI ratio is approximately 0.68, well below the commonly targeted minimum of 1.5-2.0. The developer needs to either improve monetization (higher ARPDAU) or improve retention to extend average lifetime, or find cheaper acquisition channels.

rubric.key: Reasonable LTV calculation using retention and ARPDAU
Correct conclusion that CPI exceeds LTV

questions.question: Describe the service locator pattern as applied to cross-platform mobile game development, and explain how it solves the problem of platform-specific IAP implementations on iOS and Android.
questions.answer: The service locator pattern uses a central registry that maps interface types to concrete implementations. For cross-platform IAP, you define an IPurchaseService interface with methods like PurchaseProduct() and RestorePurchases(). At startup, the game registers the appropriate concrete implementation: ApplePurchaseService on iOS (wrapping StoreKit) or GooglePurchaseService on Android (wrapping Google Play Billing Library). Game code only depends on the IPurchaseService interface and retrieves it from the service locator, so the same game logic works on both platforms without conditional compilation scattered throughout the codebase. This keeps platform-specific code isolated in the concrete service implementations.

rubric.key: Correctly describes the service locator pattern with registry concept
Explains interface abstraction for IAP across platforms
Identifies the benefit of isolating platform-specific code

major_assignments.title: Mobile-Optimized Game Prototype
major_assignments.description: Design and build a fully playable mobile game prototype that demonstrates mastery of touch input design, mobile UI patterns, and performance optimization. The game must run at a stable 60fps on a mid-range mobile device and implement at least two distinct touch interaction mechanics.
objectives: Implement intuitive touch-based controls with proper dead zones and feedback
Design a mobile-native UI that adapts to different screen sizes and safe areas
Optimize rendering and memory usage to sustain 60fps on target hardware
Integrate basic analytics to track player interaction patterns
deliverables: Unity project with iOS and Android build targets configured
APK build tested on a physical Android device
Performance profiling report showing frame times and memory usage
Technical document describing input system design decisions (2-3 pages)

criteria: Touch controls feel responsive and natural
Virtual joystick or touch areas have proper dead zones
Multi-touch interactions work without conflicts
Haptic feedback is used appropriately
UI respects safe areas on notched devices
Touch targets meet minimum size guidelines (44pt / 48dp)
UI scales correctly across different screen sizes and aspect ratios
Visual feedback for all interactive elements
Sustains 60fps on target mid-range device
Memory usage stays under 500MB
No frame spikes above 33ms during normal gameplay
Texture compression and draw call batching properly implemented
Clear explanation of input design rationale
Profiling data presented with analysis
Optimization decisions documented with before/after metrics
Professional formatting and writing
Polished touch controls that feel native to mobile
Rock-solid 60fps performance on mid-range hardware
Thoughtful mobile UI that adapts to device variations
Thorough documentation with profiling evidence

major_assignments.title: Monetized Mobile Game with Live Operations
major_assignments.description: Extend the prototype from Assignment 1 with a monetization layer and live operations infrastructure. Implement at least one IAP flow with server-side receipt validation, integrate analytics tracking for a purchase funnel, and build a remote configuration system that allows tuning game parameters without an app update.
objectives: Implement a complete IAP flow with server-side receipt validation
Integrate analytics tracking for key monetization events
Build a remote configuration system for live game tuning
Prepare an app store listing with optimized metadata and assets
deliverables: Updated Unity project with IAP and analytics integration
Server-side receipt validation endpoint (can use Firebase Functions)
App Store Connect or Google Play Console listing (sandbox/test track)
ASO analysis document with keyword research and competitor comparison (2-3 pages)

criteria: IAP flow completes successfully in sandbox environment
Receipt validation is performed server-side
Purchase restoration works correctly
Error states are handled gracefully with user feedback
Key funnel events are tracked (store view, purchase initiated, purchase completed)
Custom events provide actionable player behavior data
Analytics dashboard shows real data from test sessions
ARPDAU and retention can be calculated from tracked events
Remote config changes reflect in-game without app update
Feature flags can enable/disable game features remotely
Graceful fallback when remote config is unavailable
Configuration changes are applied without restarting the game
Keywords are researched with competitive analysis
Screenshots follow platform guidelines and show gameplay
Description is compelling and optimized for search
Professional presentation of store listing materials
Complete IAP flow with proper server-side validation
Meaningful analytics that could drive business decisions
Functional remote config for live game tuning
Professional-quality app store listing

weekly_quizzes.description: Regular assessment of mobile platform knowledge and development principles

lab_assignments.description: Hands-on exercises in touch input, performance profiling, and platform-specific integration

major_assignments.description: Two major projects covering mobile prototype development and monetization/live ops integration

midterm_examination.description: Comprehensive assessment of mobile platforms, optimization, and monetization concepts

final_project.description: Complete mobile game published to a test track with analytics, monetization, and live operations

course_policies.attendance: Attendance is mandatory for all lab sessions and device testing workshops. More than 2 unexcused absences may result in course failure.
course_policies.late_submissions: Late assignments are penalized 10% per day. No submissions accepted more than 5 days late without prior arrangement. Build submissions must compile and run on target devices.
course_policies.collaboration: Individual assignments must be completed independently. Students may share device testing resources but all code must be original or properly attributed.
course_policies.academic_integrity: All code must be original or properly attributed. Use of AI code generation tools must be disclosed. Submitting purchased or copied asset store templates as original work will result in course failure.
course_policies.technology_requirements: Students must have access to a physical iOS or Android device for testing. Lab computers have Xcode and Android Studio available. Personal Apple Developer accounts are recommended but not required.
